title: Privacy-Preserving Logs
concept: Scrubbing user credentials, tokens, and PII from execution trace logs before storing them.

exercises.prompt: What is the main reason for alerting on loop anomalies in AI systems?
exercises.explain: Alerting on loop anomalies helps in avoiding excessive resource usage by monitoring and limiting the number of iterations in a process.

cards.title: Privacy-Preserving Logs
cards.body: In AI systems, it's important to protect sensitive information. Privacy-preserving logs help us keep user data secure.

cards.title: What are Privacy-Preserving Logs?
cards.body: Privacy-preserving logs are records that exclude any personally identifiable information (PII) or sensitive credentials before being stored.

cards.title: Think of it like this!
cards.body: Imagine you have a diary with secrets. Before sharing it with a friend, you remove any names or sensitive details so your secrets stay safe.

cards.title: Example of Scrubbing Logs
cards.body: If a log records a user action that includes their email address, the scrubbed log would show only the action taken, like 'User logged in', without the email.

cards.title: Myth: Scrubbing isn't necessary
cards.body: Some people think scrubbing data isn't important, but leaving sensitive information can lead to serious privacy issues!

cards.title: Before and After Scrubbing
cards.body: See the difference in logs before and after scrubbing sensitive data.

left.text: Log: User logged in as user@example.com

exercises.prompt: What should you avoid storing in execution trace logs?

options.text: User actions taken in the system.

exercises.explain: You should avoid storing user credentials and PII in execution logs to protect user privacy and data security.

exercises.prompt: True or False: It's safe to store user passwords in execution trace logs if they are encrypted.
exercises.explain: It's never safe to store passwords, even if encrypted, as this can create security vulnerabilities.

exercises.prompt: Scrubbing logs is important to remove __________ before storing them.
exercises.sentence: Scrubbing logs is important to remove user credentials before storing them.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'user credentials,' which must be removed to protect user privacy.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of a privacy concern in logs?

options.text: Recording the CPU usage of a server.
Storing a user's email address in logs.

exercises.explain: Storing a user's email address in logs is a privacy concern because it can lead to unauthorized access to personal information.

exercises.prompt: Why is it critical to scrub logs of sensitive information before storage?

options.text: To make the logs more detailed.
To protect user privacy and prevent data breaches.
To make the logs easier to read.

exercises.explain: It's critical to protect user privacy and prevent data breaches by removing sensitive information from logs, which could be accessed by unauthorized parties.
